# Provider Runtime / Adapter SSOT: RuntimeActor Boundary

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/providerruntimeboundarydocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to provider runtime](../provider-runtime.md)

Executable SSOT: [`runtime-actor-boundary.riido.json`](runtime-actor-boundary.riido.json).

Evidence artifact: `provider-runtime-boundary-docs`.

This SSOT fixes the C4 RuntimeActor, supervisor, control-plane port, session preservation, adapter ACL, and open issue boundaries as generated documentation plus source anchors.

The boundary is intentionally local to the public daemon repository; SaaS HTTP handlers, infra, Terraform, deployment state, and private server stores are adjacent contracts, not runtime actor responsibilities.

## Executable Assertions

- RuntimeActor owns one runtime capability boundary and does not own polling, persistence, concrete provider parsing, workdir preparation, or server transport.
- ControlPlanePort is provider-neutral and splits source/reporter ports from SaaS, task DB, project, and MWSD adapters.
- Supervisor composes runtime actors, scheduling, workdir preparation, canonical event ingestion, and reporting for one daemon process.
- Provider native session identity is preserved as C4 session data and event payload, while runtime eligibility and lease fencing remain C5/C9 responsibilities.
- Provider stdin command ACLs are optional adapter-side translations from reducer commands, not provider-specific reducer branches.
- Open questions are tracked as explicit executable backlog identifiers instead of hidden narrative caveats.
